title: helianthus californicus var. parishii
intro: Helianthus californicus var. parishii, also known as Parish's sunflower, is a rare, sun-loving perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Helianthus californicus var. parishii include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used in traditional medicine to support overall well-being.
